title: cytisus nigricans subsp. atratus
intro: Cytisus nigricans subsp. atratus, also known as the dark purple broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, which have been shown to provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, offering potential health benefits including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties. Cytisus nigricans subsp. atratus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
